nodes.context: Colombian criminal organization, classified as GAO, dedicated to drug trafficking and other illegal activities.
Colombian criminal group, classified as GAO, formed from dissidents of the EPL guerrilla.
Colombian guerrilla group that demobilized in 2016; some members formed dissident groups (GAOR).
Mexican drug cartel with ties to Colombian criminal groups.
Mexican criminal organization, described as dismantled in 2016.
Mexican criminal organization that since 2018 seeks to control cocaine production and distribution in Colombia.
Colombian paramilitary group that demobilized in 2006; some members later rearmed to form Bacrim.
Colombian criminal organization, one of the main Bacrim, weakened by captures.

nodes.standard_name: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
nodes.original_text_name: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
nodes.context: Colombian criminal organization, considered one of the most important Bacrim.
Colombian criminal group, classified as Bacrim.
Colombian criminal organization, classified as Bacrim.
Colombian criminal organization, classified as Bacrim.
Minor Colombian criminal faction, classified as Bacrim.
Minor Colombian criminal faction, classified as Bacrim.
Colombian drug cartel; Los Rastrojos founder was linked to it.
Largest criminal organization in Colombia, with over 2000 members, involved in drug trafficking and other crimes.
Formerly a large criminal group, reduced to small cells and dismantled by 2016.
Disintegrated group, with dissident factions Bloque Meta and Bloque Libertadores del Vichada that later reunified as Los Puntilleros.
Reunified dissident group from ERPAC, dismantled in 2017.
Insurgent group, but its dissidence (Los Pelusos) is classified as a criminal band due to drug trafficking.
Dissident faction of the EPL, classified as a criminal band, active in Catatumbo.
Local criminal group operating in the Medellín metropolitan area.
Local criminal group in Putumayo.
Local criminal group in Pereira.
Local criminal group in Santa Marta, with about 500 members.
Dissident group of the Clan del Golfo, active in Bajo Cauca.
Mexican drug cartel with presence in Colombia since 2017.
Mexican drug cartel with presence in Colombia since 2017.
GAOR (residual armed group) that was the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel in Colombia.
Dissident factions of the FARC-EP that did not demobilize, considered criminal bands.
Guerrilla group declared as GAO by the Duque government, involved in conflicts and alliances.
Cartel formed by corrupt members of the Venezuelan government, involved in drug trafficking.
Dissident faction of the FARC-EP led by Iván Márquez, allied with the Cartel de los Soles.

edges.evidence_quote: El Clan Úsuga y Los Rastrojos eran reconocidas como las dos grandes agrupaciones que han logrado una expansión territorial considerable...
edges.context: The text describes them as the two main groups competing for territorial control.

edges.evidence_quote: situación aprovechada por el 'Clan Úsuga', que ha reclutado a exmiembros de los Rastrojos y Los Paisas para aumentar su pie de fuerza
edges.context: Clan Úsuga recruited former members of Los Paisas.

edges.evidence_quote: por vincularlo a los negocios del Cartel del Norte del Valle , ser uno de los principales traficantes de drogas y ser el fundador de Los Rastrojos.
edges.context: The founder of Los Rastrojos was linked to the Norte del Valle Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: varias están enfrentadas entre sí por control territorial de actividades ilegales, incluso algunas trabajan con el Cártel de Sinaloa y el Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación
edges.context: Some FARC dissident groups work with the Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: varias están enfrentadas entre sí por control territorial de actividades ilegales, incluso algunas trabajan con el Cártel de Sinaloa y el Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación
edges.context: Some FARC dissident groups work with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: El primero, miembro de un grupo de criminales que anteriormente estaba al servicio del ex paramilitar Daniel Rendón Herrera , conocido por Don Mario , que inició las actividades de lo que se conoció después por la opinión pública colombiana como narcoparamilitarismo.
edges.context: Clan del Golfo originated from former AUC members who rearmed.

edges.evidence_quote: Luis Fernando era señalado por múltiples delitos, entre ellos, en la participación y posterior fundación de la bacrim Los Paisas , en complicidad con otros individuos.
edges.context: Los Paisas was founded by a former AUC member.

edges.target: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
edges.evidence_quote: Pedro Oliviero Guerrero Castillo , alias Cuchillo , exjefe del Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia (ERPAC)
edges.context: ERPAC was formed by former AUC members who did not demobilize.

edges.evidence_quote: varias están enfrentadas entre sí por control territorial de actividades ilegales, incluso algunas trabajan con el Cártel de Sinaloa y el Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación
edges.context: Some FARC dissident groups work with the Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: varias están enfrentadas entre sí por control territorial de actividades ilegales, incluso algunas trabajan con el Cártel de Sinaloa y el Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación
edges.context: Some FARC dissident groups work with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: las deserciones de sus miembros a su otrora rival "Clan del Golfo"
edges.context: Members of Los Rastrojos defected to the Clan del Golfo, indicating a cooperative transfer of personnel.

edges.evidence_quote: una guerra entre el Clan del Golfo y su grupo disidente "Los Caparrapos"
edges.context: The Clan del Golfo is at war with its dissident group Los Caparrapos.

edges.evidence_quote: Guacho era el enlace con el Cártel de Sinaloa de México y su grupo era el brazo armado de este cartel en Colombia.
edges.context: The Frente Oliver Sinisterra acted as the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel in Colombia.

edges.evidence_quote: afectados por la guerra que sostienen con el ELN en la región
edges.context: Los Pelusos are at war with the ELN in the Catatumbo region.

edges.evidence_quote: alianzas con la disidencia de las FARC-EP Segunda Marquetalia, al mando de alias Iván Márquez, quien estaría realizando con sus lugartenientes negocios de narcotráfico con el Cártel de los Soles
edges.context: Segunda Marquetalia is allied with the Cartel de los Soles for drug trafficking.
